title: mark_session_start
description: Open a new work session anchored to a stated intent and return a freshly generated session id. Captures the user's own framing of what they are about to do so later interventions can quote it back verbatim.

intent.description: Plain-language statement of what the user intends to do in this session. Stored verbatim. Surfaced unmodified by hyperfocus nudges and end-of-session reflection.
examples: write the migration ADR and stop at 1 page

session_id.description: UUIDv4 identifier for the new session. Stable for the session's lifetime; opaque to callers.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset for when the server recorded the session start.

intent.description: The intent as stored, echoed back unmodified so the caller can verify what was recorded.

auto_closed_prior_session.description: If a prior session was still open when this one started, this field MAY describe the action taken. The exact policy (auto-close vs error) is an open question tracked in ADR 0001; null when not applicable.

INTENT_REQUIRED.description: The intent argument was missing or empty after trimming. Sessions without stated intent defeat the purpose of this tool.

INTENT_TOO_LONG.description: Intent exceeded 500 characters. Encourage the caller to summarise; long intents are themselves a hyperfocus signal.

SESSION_ALREADY_OPEN.description: Returned only if the server policy is configured to error on overlapping sessions rather than auto-close. See ADR 0001 open question.

examples.name: typical start with no prior session
start that auto-closes a forgotten prior session

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. Adding the optional output field 'auto_closed_prior_session' is itself an example of the kind of additive change permitted. Removing or retyping any field requires a new major.
compatibility.side_effects: Mutates server-local session state. NOT idempotent — calling twice creates two sessions.
compatibility.idempotency: Not idempotent. Callers SHOULD NOT retry blindly on network errors; instead they should query session state first.
compatibility.privacy: Intent strings are stored locally only. They are sensitive (they reveal user goals and emotional framing) and MUST NOT be transmitted off-device by this server.
